# The New Sacristy of San Lorenzo before Michelangelo Howard SaalmanCarnegie-Mellon UniversityPittsburgh, PA15213
Pages 199-228|Published online: 14 Aug 2014  ...    This paper reproposes the question of whether Michelangelo began the New Sacristy of San Lorenzo*ex novo*or whether he inserted his design into a preexistent structure on the site. Examination of the urban topography around the western end of San Lorenzo after 1400, of the basement story and the exterior of the sacristy, and of published and unpublished documents leads to the conclusion that a mausoleum-sacristy project, initiated around 14

In his book on Tuscan marble carving, Francis Ames-Lewis reminds us that the duties of the head of a workshop included responsibility for the acquisition of materials, workshop management and quality control: three areas in which time-keeping was crucial. 1 The amount of time needed to carry out the variety of  ...    others' work or to solve problems encountered by assistants' was not easy to predict. The first of these tasks is often illustrated by citing the case of Nicola Pisano, who undertook the lengthy process of choosing blocks from the Carrara quarry himself, and had them transported to Pisa for the cathedral pulpit of Siena in 1265. 2 At other times, workers were assigned this job, as when the Opera del Duomo in Florence decided on 26 June 1355 that a man named Domenicho should go to Pisa to take charge of ships, then to Avenza to collect stone. 3 Problems in  When Michelangelo
